channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: An Alabama woman is doing well after the latest experimental pig kidney transplant
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — An Alabama woman is recovering well after a pig kidney transplant last month that freed her from eight years of dialysis, the latest effort to save human lives with animal organs.
item.dc:creator: LAURAN NEERGAARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Prosecutors to announce 'significant development' in Gilgo Beach killings investigation
item.description: RIVERHEAD, N.Y. (AP) — The New York architect charged in a string of deaths known as the Gilgo Beach killings is set to appear in court on Tuesday, with prosecutors saying they will announce a “significant development" in the investigation.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Pennsylvania's Bethlehem: The city founded by Moravians on Christmas eve keeps its traditions alive
item.description: BETHLEHEM, Pa. (AP) — On Christmas Eve in 1741, Moravian settlers named this Pennsylvania city after the biblical birthplace of Jesus. Nearly 300 years later, Moravians continue celebrating their Christmas season traditions in Bethlehem.
item.dc:creator: LUIS ANDRES HENAO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Americans end 2024 with grim economic outlook, but Republicans are optimistic for 2025: AP-NORC poll
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The unemployment rate is healthy and the stock market is up, but Democrats are feeling more pessimistic about the U.S. economy after Donald Trump's election victory, according to a new poll.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK and LINLEY SANDERS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: The Latest: Teacher and a student killed in a shooting at a private Christian school in Wisconsin
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — A teenage student opened fire at the Abundant Life Christian School in Madison, Wisconsin on Monday, killing a teacher and another teenage student in the final week before Christmas break. The shooter also died, police said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US Customs and Border Protection pilot killed in helicopter crash near San Diego
item.description: A U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP) pilot died Monday after the crash of a helicopter near San Diego and the southern border, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mystery drone sightings continue in New Jersey and across the US. Here's what we know
item.description: TRENTON, N.J. (AP) — A large number of mysterious drones have been reported flying over New Jersey and across the eastern U.S., sparking speculation and concern over where they came from and why. New Jersey Gov. Phil Murphy and U.S. Sen.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Lt. Governor says Texas may purchase border wall material auctioned by federal government
item.description: The Texas Facilities Commission said the materials up for sale were "mostly junk, with most panels covered in concrete and rust.
item.dc:creator: Dylan McKim, NewsNation

item.title: Senate Democrats push plan to abolish Electoral College
item.description: Three Democratic senators unveiled a constitutional amendment to abolish the Electoral College system Monday, just more than a month after President-elect Trump stunned the Democrats by sweeping all seven battleground states, knocking off three Senate Democratic incumbents in the process. Sens.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Bud Light no longer the best-selling draft beer at bars, data suggests
item.description: The overtaking of Bud Light as America's most popular draft beer has been "years in the making," according to the founder of Draftline Technologies.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, NewsNation

item.title: Bill to officially make bald eagle national bird heads to Biden’s desk
item.description: A bill to officially designate the bald eagle as the national bird will head to President Biden’s desk after passage in the House on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: March for Our Lives co-founder David Hogg launches DNC vice chair bid
item.description: Hogg, 24, cited Democrats’ losing ground with young voters in November as a motivating factor to run for the leadership position within the party.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, NewsNation

item.title: Watch: Biden speaks at Labor Department
item.description: President Biden spoke early Monday afternoon at the Labor Department to highlight his administration's efforts to strengthen America's workforce.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump, Softbank CEO announce $100B SoftBank investment in the US
item.description: resident-elect Donald Trump will join SoftBank Group CEO Masayoshi Son at his Florida home on Monday to announce that the Japanese company is planning to invest $100 billion in U.S. projects over the next four years.
item.dc:creator: Colleen Long, KRON4

item.title: Supreme Court won’t block order directing Peter Navarro to turn over emails
item.description: The Supreme Court on Monday declined to weigh Peter Navarro’s bid to reverse a court order directing him to turn over hundreds of emails sent during his tenure as an adviser in the first Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Jill Jacobson, actress who appeared on 'Star Trek' and 'Falcon Crest,' dies at 70
item.description: "We are incredibly sad to say goodbye to our beautiful, soulful, hysterically funny, elegantly raunchy client, Jill Jacobson," her manager Ben Padua told Nexstar.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Paris Hilton heading to Capitol with 'urgent' plea to pass youth welfare program reform
item.description: Paris Hilton is heading back to the Capitol with an urgent plea, calling on "every member of the House" to pass a bill focused on youth congregate care facilities that she says would "create real, meaningful change" before the legislative session ends this week.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, NewsNation

item.title: McConnell urges Trump to reject isolationism
item.description: Outgoing Senate Republican Leader Mitch McConnell (R-Ky.) is calling on President-elect Trump to reject isolationists in the Republican Party who will argue for the United States to focus on China at the expense of Ukraine and other areas of the world.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Report documents family separations during 1st Trump term
item.description: A report examining years of family separation policies enacted under the Trump administration is being released Monday by several migrant advocacy nonprofit organizations to remind the public and lawmakers of what transpired, Border Report has learned.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5

item.title: As schools cut back on bus service, parents turn to rideshare apps
item.description: Reliance on school buses has been waning for years as districts struggle to find drivers and more students attend schools far outside their neighborhoods.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 2 teens killed, 4 injured in shooting at makeshift club in Houston
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — Two teens were killed and four were injured — including a 13-year-old girl — in a shooting at a makeshift club in Houston, police said Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Takeaways from AP's reporting on a child sex abuse crisis inside a secretive Christian sect
item.description: BOISE, Idaho (AP) — For decades, a little-known Christian sect has dealt with its abusive ministers and spiritual elders mostly in secret, shunning legal action in favor of urging victims to forgive the predators in their midst.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Camp Lejeune survivor: EPA not doing enough on cancer-causing chemicals
item.description: Retired Marine Corps Master Sergeant Jerry Ensminger said how the EPA must be helped to rid America of forever chemicals.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Former longtime South Carolina congressman John Spratt dies at 82
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — John Spratt, a former longtime Democratic congressman from South Carolina who successfully pushed for a balanced budget deal in the 1990s but was unseated decades later when his district turned Republican, has died. He was 82.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER and MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Thousands of mushrooms, arsenal of guns, grenades seized from Oregon home
item.description: Charges are pending against 50-year-old Oregon resident Matthew Houglum and other suspected co-conspirators following the raid.
item.dc:creator: John Ross Ferrara, NewsNation

item.title: California man dies after being mauled by his own dogs: police
item.description: A man was mauled by his three dogs at a neighborhood park Thursday afternoon, according to the San Diego Police Department.
item.dc:creator: Tony Shin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: The original lyrics of 'Have Yourself a Merry Little Christmas' were extremely depressing
item.description: “Have Yourself a Merry Little Christmas” is already more melancholy than most classic Christmas songs, but its earlier version was a downright downer.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4

item.title: Millionaire boomers less likely to share their wealth: Charles Schwab
item.description: Data revealed that baby boomers, born from 1946 to 1964, plan to spend most of their wealth rather than reserve it for future generations.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: ABC settles Trump’s defamation suit for $15M
item.description: The settlement enables both the anchor and Trump to avoid sitting for court-ordered depositions next week.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KRON4

item.title: Texas man gets 100 years in prison for shootings that killed 1, wounded others in Nevada, Arizona
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — A judge in Las Vegas sentenced a Texas man to 100 years in prison for his role in a two-state shooting rampage on Thanksgiving 2020 that included the killing of a man in Nevada and a shootout with authorities in Arizona.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Texas AG sues New York doctor for providing abortion pills across state lines
item.description: Both surgical and medication abortions are banned in Texas, and Friday’s lawsuit is the first legal test to see what happens when state abortion laws conflict with one another.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra O'Connell-Domenech, WFLA, NBC

item.title: A look back at the most notable bankruptcies of 2024
item.description: This year saw its fair share of bankruptcies, with household names and century-old companies in the mix.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, Fox 5

item.title: UK hockey team retires Adam Johnson's No. 47 to honor player who died after neck cut
item.description:
Adam Johnson made as big an impression off the ice as he did on it for the Nottingham Panthers.
The U.K. hockey team officially retired Johnson's No. 47 jersey on Saturday in tribute to the American forward who died after his neck was cut by an opponent’s skate during a game over a year ago.
item.dc:creator: KEN MAGUIRE, Associated Press, Fox 5
